reasoning: The Assessment Guide explicitly notes that IR.L2-3.6.3 tests the organizational incident response capability. That capability must first be established under IR.L2-3.6.1 before there is anything to test. You cannot validate a capability that does not yet exist — testing presupposes an operational IR capability as its subject.

Incident reporting (IR.L2-3.6.2) depends on the operational handling capability (IR.L2-3.6.1) to generate the incidents that are tracked, documented, and reported. Specifically, IR.L2-3.6.1's analysis phase produces the incident determination that triggers IR.L2-3.6.2's reporting obligations. The tracking system and contact lists defined in IR.L2-3.6.1 are also the mechanisms used to satisfy IR.L2-3.6.2's tracking and notification objectives.

Testing (IR.L2-3.6.3) directly improves the handling capability (IR.L2-3.6.1) through the after-action process. Deficiencies found during exercises drive updates to plans, playbooks, training, and tools — each of which strengthens a specific phase of the IR.L2-3.6.1 capability. An IR capability that is never tested drifts from current threats and organizational changes; testing is what keeps 3.6.1 current.
